text: During a low anterior resection, the surgeon uses a robotic endo-wrist stapler to clamp the tissue. As the firing process begins, an alert indicates a stapler malfunction, with the blade potentially exposed and the transaction incomplete. The surgeon removes the stapler to inspect it, discovering a fallen piece of the instrument within the patient's body, which is then retrieved.

The surgeon uses a single-port approach to perform anterior resection, employing one grip for traction on the mesentery and an endo-wrist stapler to ligate the inferior mesenteric artery. After firing the stapler, it is observed that the ureter has been accidentally transected, highlighting the need for careful separation and protection of the ureter during the procedure.

During the dissection of locally advanced rectal cancer, presacral bleeding occurs. The surgical team attempts to secure the bleeding points using packing and compression methods, but the bleeding persists, leading to a conversion to open surgery.
